ogTitle: McGraths Hill vs McKellars Park: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in McGraths Hill and McKellars Park.
heading: McGraths Hill vs McKellars Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between McGraths Hill, New South Wales and McKellars Park, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: McGraths Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (43/100) compared to McKellars Park (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is McGraths Hill or McKellars Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: McGraths Hill has a flood risk score of 43/100, while McKellars Park scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: McGraths Hill scores 44/100 for bushfire risk. McKellars Park scores 58/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in McGraths Hill or McKellars Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. McGraths Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
